stories.headline: Japan's industrial production and retail sales improve despite Iran war
Death Toll Rises in Washington Paper Mill Explosion
stories.summary: At least eight are now confirmed dead, and firefighters believe there are three more bodies they have yet to reach, making the crisis the worst industrial disaster in the state’s modern history.

stories.headline: Asia security summit opens with US strategy in focus amid Iran war
Japan’s Two-Year Bond Yield Rises After Lukewarm Auction
stories.summary: Japan’s two-year government bond yield edged higher after an auction of the tenor saw slightly weak demand as investors bet on a near-term interest rate hike by the Bank of Japan.

stories.headline: Ferdinand Marcos Jr Addresses Spat with VP Duterte
stories.summary: The Philippine President's uneasy alliance with his Vice President has escalated into a second impeachment for Sara Duterte in as many years, and chaos within the Senate. Marcos shares his views on the rivalry between the two families in an exclusive interview with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin on "Latitude". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Australia Secures First Cargo Ship for Strategic Maritime Fleet
stories.summary: Australia has secured the first vessel for its long-awaited maritime strategic fleet program, designed to help the country respond to supply-chain disruptions such as those caused by the war in the Middle East, as well as natural disasters and other crises.

stories.headline: 3 Dead After Large Fire at Dallas Apartment Building
stories.summary: A damaged gas line led to a five-alarm fire in the Oak Cliff neighborhood. At least four were injured, and firefighters believe they may find more victims.

stories.headline: At least three dead after fire destroys Dallas apartment complex
stories.summary: A large fire destroyed an apartment complex in Dallas after crews responded to reports of a gas leak.

stories.headline: How China is breaking apart a people and its culture
stories.summary: The campaign of repression against the Uyghurs has entered a new phase

stories.headline: Don’t bet on Trump reining in the prediction markets

stories.headline: Several BP shareholders found ousted chair ‘challenging’
stories.summary: Several large investors say it was hard to arrange meetings with Albert Manifold before his removal from oil major

stories.headline: Aggressive Asia Hikes Show Few Signs of Stabilizing Currencies
stories.summary: Asian central banks are turning to ever more aggressive interest-rate hikes to shore up currencies, though there are few signs of success.

stories.headline: Iron Ore Heads for Monthly Loss as Coal Spike Pressures Margins
stories.summary: Iron ore headed for a monthly loss as a short-lived rally triggered by a fatal steelmaking-coal mine accident in China’s Shanxi province faded, leaving investors focused on mill margins and mounting shipments.

stories.headline: Global Stocks Rally on Hopes of US-Iran Deal | The Asia Trade 5/29/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Sydney with Paul Allen and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Japan Can Act on Currency If There’s Volatility, Katayama Says
stories.summary: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama reiterated that authorities can step into the foreign exchange market if there’s volatility or evidence of speculative moves, in comments ahead of data expected to confirm authorities intervened at some point during the past month.

stories.headline: Credit Agricole Taps Japan Samurai Market at Wider Spread
stories.summary: Credit Agricole SA set terms for ¥106.5 billion ($670 million) of samurai bonds, offering wider spreads than comparable domestic corporate debt amid rising benchmark yen rates.

stories.headline: Japan Banks See Hybrid Bond Boom to Fund Regulatory Capital
stories.summary: Japan’s major banks are set for their busiest fiscal year in over a decade for issuing a special category of bonds that will finance higher capital requirements.

stories.headline: China Export Prices Climb Most in Three Years on Oil Shock
stories.summary: China’s export prices rose at the sharpest pace in three years, as a global oil shock filters through the world’s manufacturing powerhouse and an AI investment boom sends chip prices soaring.

stories.headline: New Zealand Firms Slightly Less Gloomy as Costs Remain Elevated
stories.summary: New Zealand business sentiment recovered slightly in May, but remains near three-year lows as surging fuel prices slow economic growth, damping customer demand and eroding profits.

stories.headline: US Treasury secretary confirms plans for banknote featuring Trump’s face
stories.summary: Proposed $250 bill would mark the first time a living person has appeared on US currency in more than a century.

stories.headline: Hong Kong and Singapore brace for AI chill in finance job market
Humanoid robots strut their stuff as trade show comes to Japan
US and Iran reach tentative deal, pending Trump's approval
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to extend the ceasefire by 60 days to make space for further negotiations to end the war but Trump still has to approve the deal, reports said Thursday, citing US officials familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: EU sanctions ‘extremist’ Israeli settlers in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: European Union says sanctioned individuals and groups have violated the rights of Palestinians.

stories.headline: Israel’s Netanyahu directs army to seize 70 percent of Gaza Strip
stories.summary: Israeli army has already expanded its control of Gaza by 11 percent over the 'Yellow Line', beyond terms of 'ceasefire'.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: After stern words from Trump, U.S. and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: The renewed violence came after the president said Iran was wrong if it thought he would feel pressure to make concessions and resolve the standoff before U.S. midterm elections later this year.

stories.headline: EU will never be a neutral mediator between Ukraine and Russia, says Kallas
stories.summary: The High Representative has dismissed the buzz over a special envoy for talks with Russia, arguing the EU will "never" be a neutral mediator between Moscow and Kyiv. "We have been clearly on Ukraine's side," she said.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders Israeli army to seize 70 percent of Gaza
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he has ordered the military to take control of 70 percent of Gaza.

stories.headline: Duterte ICC trial to start on 30 November: What you need to know

stories.headline: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
stories.summary: Fibre-optic drones are now Hezbollah's primary weapon against Israel's soldiers and civilians.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Stocks Rise to Record, Oil Slips on Iran Optimism: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks scaled to a record and oil dropped after the US and Iran reached a tentative deal to extend their ceasefire, pending President Donald Trump’s signoff.

stories.headline: Clashes between armed groups in Colombia kill at least 52
stories.summary: Rival groups are vying for territorial control of strategic cocaine production and trafficking regionAt least 52 guerrilla fighters have been killed in clashes between two rival armed groups vying for territorial control of a strategic cocaine production and trafficking region in south-east Colombia, a faction of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) involved in the fighting has said.The clashes, the most violent in recent months, too

stories.headline: Guatemala denies agreeing to US strikes against drug traffickers
stories.summary: President Bernardo Arevalo's government says it requested security cooperation but did not approve US attacks.

stories.headline: French parliament inches towards symbolic repeal of 'Code noir' slavery legislation
stories.summary: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."

stories.headline: Australia sues US giant 3M over 'forever chemicals' in firefighting foam
stories.summary: The A$2bn case, which centres on contamination at defence sites, is the largest ever brought by the government.

stories.headline: Canada signs landmark LNG energy deal with Germany
stories.summary: The deal would see one million tons of natural gas shipped annually from Canada to Germany by the early 2030s through a proposed terminal in British Columbia.

stories.headline: Musk’s tweet undermines SpaceX’s claims about Anthropic data centre deal

stories.headline: Nomura Raises Annual Profit Target 50% After Record Year
stories.summary: Nomura Holdings Inc. raised its midterm profit targets following a year of record earnings, as Chief Executive Officer Kentaro Okuda pushes for stable growth at Japan’s biggest brokerage.

stories.headline: China’s Troubled Consumer Funds Turn to Tech Stock Pickers
stories.summary: China’s battered consumer-focused funds are showing signs of pivoting to tech as a prolonged demand slump forces even the sector’s most committed backers to rethink their strategy.

stories.headline: Swallowing Concerns, G.O.P. Senators Rally Behind Paxton
stories.summary: After attacking the Texas attorney general, who won the G.O.P. nomination, Senate Republicans are pivoting sharply to support him in what is now a competitive race.

stories.headline: Memphis Residents Sue Federal Agents Over Arrest Tactics Amid Crime and Immigration Crackdown
stories.summary: A federal task force in the city has been targeting crime and undocumented immigrants since October. Observers say they have faced retaliation for trying to track the agents’ work.

stories.headline: Michigan’s Whitmer walks back comment saying she won’t run for president in 2028
stories.summary: Whitmer, term-limited after two gubernatorial wins in her battleground state, has been widely viewed as a potential Democratic candidate.

stories.headline: What are Republicans going to campaign on?
stories.summary: Trump is unpopular. Gas prices are high. Republicans are searching for a midterm message.

stories.headline: Family accuse UK government of lack of support over death of Briton in Grenada

stories.headline: Image of Thai police in sparkly dresses with handcuffed suspect turns out to be AI fake
stories.summary: Picture was created by administrator in charge of station’s Facebook account who wanted to create ‘friendlier image’It was an arresting image and an irresistible story. A group of tough Thai police officers – five men and one woman – all wearing elaborate festival-style dresses, surrounding a drug dealer they had caught while undercover.The image, released by local police, was so compelling that it found its way on to the front page of the UK’s D

stories.headline: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs
stories.summary: When an Inner Mongolia farmer sought two herders to tend his 3,000 sheep, he was swamped with applicants including graduates, factory labourers and white-collar workersA Chinese farm owner’s recruitment drive for shepherds has ended in success after his job advert seeking people to work on his Inner Mongolia ranch went viral, drawing the attention of city dwellers struggling to find work and highlighting growing strains in China’s labour market.Z

stories.headline: ‘Hidden datacentre tax’ costing Irish households millions, report says
stories.summary: Datacentres used 22% of country’s electricity last year, pushing up household bills, study suggestsEnergy demand by datacentres in Ireland has added hundreds of euros to household electricity bills in a pattern that could be replicated across Europe, according to a report.Ireland’s growing number of datacentres last year used 22% of the country’s electricity, more than all urban homes combined, according to the Central Statistics Office. stories.summary: Indians celebrate Eid al-Adha amid prayer restrictions.

stories.headline: What’s behind South Africa’s anti-migrant protests?
stories.summary: Thousands of foreign workers are under increasing pressure to leave the country.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing

stories.headline: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran deal yet
stories.summary: The US president says he believes Iran wants to reach a deal but there is still no agreement on its terms.

stories.headline: Almost 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine war, GCHQ says

stories.summary: Saudi Arabia says that is 11,000 more than the number of Muslims who travelled to the Gulf kingdom last year.

stories.headline: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
stories.summary: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
stories.summary: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.

stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: ‘Long live Raúl!’ - Castro’s supporters in Cuba rally in his defence
